data.title: Brivo Announces UK Expansion, Showcases AI-First Unified Security at The Security Event
data.description: Brivo, a leading AI innovator in cloud-native physical security, has recently established a regional office in London to better cater to its expanding customer network in the UK and Ireland. The initiative aims to provide comprehensive support to partners and customers, encompassing local sales, marketing, technical assistance, sales engineering, and customer success. The new London office features specialized areas for meetings, demonstrations, and training sessions, underscoring Brivo's commitment to offering all-encompassing services.

data.title: Kitron to expand Swedish manufacturing footprint
data.description: In April 2026, Kitron announced its intention to construct a bigger establishment for its subsidiary, Kitron Eltech (previously known as DeltaNordic), in Örnsköldsvik, Sweden. This decision stems from the anticipation of substantial growth in the foreseeable future, largely attributed to defense projects. The expansion plan is aimed at meeting the escalating demands, and signifies a strategic move by Kitron to fortify its presence in the region.

data.title: 30th CUPE local headed to the picket line amid province wide long term care strike
data.description: More than 100 employees at Lunenburg Home for Special Care in Nova Scotia, represented by Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) Local 4919, have issued a 48-hour notice to strike. They may commence picketing as soon as Saturday, April 25, as part of the ongoing province-wide strike in the long-term care sector. This marks the 30th local chapter to take job action. Christa Sweeney stated, "We have been eager to return to negotiations since the start of the strike."

data.title: The Steadfastness of a 13-Year Veteran Exchange: Understanding the Risk Control Logic Behind HTX Earn
data.description: In Panama City, Florida, on April 24, 2026, amid the volatile crypto market, investors are shifting their focus from "where to trade" to prioritizing the safety of their assets. Ensuring the security of investments has emerged as a key consideration in capital allocation decisions. This shift reflects the current climate of uncertainty within the crypto space.

data.title: HOYA BIT Becomes World's First BSI ISO 14068-1 Certified Carbon-Neutral Crypto Exchange
data.description: In a recent announcement on April 24, 2026, HOYA BIT, a cryptocurrency exchange company based in Taiwan, declared a significant achievement. The company has successfully obtained the BSI ISO 14068-1 certification for carbon neutrality, making it the pioneer in the realm of cryptocurrency exchanges worldwide. This accolade underscores HOYA BIT's commitment to environmental sustainability in its operations. Noteworthy figures involved in this milestone include Bryn Sutton, Vice President of BSI, and Aiman Ali, the Global Head of the organization.
